title: ambrosia monogyra
intro: Ambrosia monogyra, also known as the annual ragweed, is a plant species in the sunflower family native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Ambrosia monogyra include its ability to treat digestive issues, relieve respiratory problems, and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of Ambrosia monogyra typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
